decision: Fetch discussions via GraphQL POST .../graphql (fixed query), item_type=discussion
reasoning: GitHub Discussions has no REST endpoint — GraphQL is mandatory; kickoff + PROJECT_SPEC §8

decision: Approach A: inclusion by createdAt within lookback_days, same as issues
reasoning: user-confirmed Approach A at kickoff; simplest, mirrors issue path; no ordering-vs-inclusion skew
rejected: hotness/activity ranking by updatedAt or comment count (deferred — PROJECT_SPEC 'Discussion nóng' future scope)

decision: Discussions-disabled repo (data.repository.discussions==null + errors) → WARN + empty list, run continues
reasoning: not all repos enable Discussions; must degrade gracefully like a 404 repo, never crash

decision: item_id = discussion number (stringified), not GraphQL global node id
reasoning: mirrors issue mapping AC-2-016; renders as #42 not opaque base64; number stable per repo

decision: Cursor pagination via pageInfo.hasNextPage/endCursor, reuse max_items_per_repo/page_size
reasoning: GraphQL uses cursors not Link header; reuse existing config tunables, no new field

decision: Order CREATED_AT DESC; early-stop when createdAt < cutoff
reasoning: inclusion and ordering both key on createdAt → exact early-stop, no v2-003-style skew

decision: 200 with non-disabled/non-not-found top-level errors → raise clear error, not silent empty
reasoning: GraphQL 200-with-errors model; RATE_LIMITED/query errors must not be silently dropped
rejected: treat all 200 as success (REST assumption — would hide real errors)

decision: Fixed non-mutating query; only owner/name/cursor/pageSize as variables
reasoning: no injection surface; never build query from untrusted input; never a mutation

decision: fetch_discussions adapter-only; GitHubClient Protocol unchanged; no state/LLM access
reasoning: mirrors v2-003 BR-V2-003-003 / summarizer-llm-4 ADR-005; keep Protocol frozen
rejected: add fetch_discussions to the Protocol (breaks frozen contract)

decision: No digest-renderer delta — renderer already emits ### Discussion (N)
reasoning: GROUP_ORDER already includes 'discussion' (AC-5-006/AC-5-011); a MODIFIED delta would be a no-op rejected by openspec validate; memory lesson from v2-003
rejected: write a no-op renderer MODIFIED delta (kickoff note suggested one)

decision: Reuse transport-level HTTP classification unchanged; only GraphQL payload error model is new
reasoning: _classify handles 429/5xx/auth on the POST; only the 200-with-errors payload layer is added
rejected: duplicate transport retry logic for GraphQL

decision: CONFIRMED at SPEC-LOCK: item_id = discussion number (stringified), not GraphQL global node id
reasoning: user-confirmed at SPEC-LOCK 2026-07-09; was ASSUMED — now locked; mirrors issue mapping, renders #42

decision: CONFIRMED at SPEC-LOCK: order CREATED_AT DESC, early-stop when createdAt < cutoff
reasoning: user-confirmed at SPEC-LOCK 2026-07-09; was ASSUMED — now locked; inclusion+ordering share createdAt so no skew

decision: Cursor loop with per-item early-stop on createdAt < cutoff, bounded by max_items_per_repo
reasoning: inclusion and ordering both key on createdAt → exact early-stop, no ordering-vs-inclusion skew (v2-003 RISK-002 does not recur); mirrors fetch_items control-flow
rejected: page-level stop (over-fetches a page past cutoff)

decision: Generalize _request_with_retry with keyword json_body: None→GET (REST unchanged), dict→POST (GraphQL only)
reasoning: one retry/classify/token path shared verbatim (BR-V2-006-010); scopes GET-only to REST, adds one fixed POST; minimal diff
rejected: verb-agnostic Request sender (over-general, weakens fixed-query invariant)

decision: Shape-first ordered 200-payload classification: null repository/discussions→skip; else non-empty errors→raise (RateLimitError for RATE_LIMITED else CollectorError); else map
reasoning: RISK-003 3-way from one 200; keying on null connection SHAPE is robust to GitHub error wording; null-check BEFORE errors-raise since disabled repo carries both
rejected: match errors[].type string (brittle, hardcodes assumed string)
treat any errors as skip-repo (silently drops real errors, violates AC-014)

decision: Map markdown `body` (not bodyText); item_id=str(number); node missing number→skip
reasoning: body is markdown the summarizer/renderer expect; number renders #42 and mirrors issue mapping (AC-005 locked)

decision: No openapi.yaml — CLI tool, no HTTP API exposed; document internal fetch_discussions contract in design §API Design
reasoning: R5 openapi is conditional on having an API; consistent with all prior changes (v2-003 ADR-004)
rejected: author openapi describing the outbound GitHub GraphQL call (misrepresents consumed API as exposed)

decision: Reuse CollectorError hierarchy: transport _classify unchanged on POST; payload RATE_LIMITED→RateLimitError, other GraphQL errors→CollectorError; no new error class
reasoning: per-module one-error-class convention; RateLimitError routes to pipeline partial-deliver arm consistently

decision: json_body=None default keeps existing REST callers on GET; dict→POST (GraphQL only); shared retry/classify loop unchanged
reasoning: ADR-002 exactly as designed; confirmed by test_fetch_items_still_issues_get_adr002_regression and test_fetch_releases_still_issues_get_adr002_regression

decision: _classify_graphql returns _GraphQLOutcome.SKIP_REPO sentinel or the discussions connection dict (not just nodes list)
reasoning: returning the connection dict keeps responsibility clean — classifier returns the relevant payload fragment; cursor loop extracts nodes+pageInfo; semantically equivalent to design's list[dict] return

decision: _classify_graphql return type: connection dict instead of list[dict] as shown in design pseudocode
reasoning: design pseudocode simplified to list[dict] but the cursor loop needs pageInfo too; returning the full connection is the correct interpretation
rejected: returning only nodes list (would require restructuring classify to also return pageInfo separately)

decision: ADR-003 null-shape BEFORE errors-raise order implemented and pinned by test_disabled_null_shape_detected_BEFORE_errors_raise
reasoning: active concern from handoff; a dedicated test asserts a payload with BOTH null connection AND non-RATE_LIMITED errors → SKIP_REPO (not raise)
rejected: checking errors first (would crash run on disabled-Discussions repo)

decision: Inner guard mirrors release guard exactly: except (InvalidRepoError, NetworkError); except CollectorError with isinstance(exc,(AuthError,RateLimitError)):raise exclusion
reasoning: v2-003 memory lesson: AuthError⊂CollectorError must never be swallowed by inner guard; fatal/terminal errors must reach outer arms
rejected: bare except CollectorError without fatal exclusion

decision: Log format extended to include issues/releases/discussions counts per repo
reasoning: additive — more diagnostic value; existing test asserts only that repo names appear in logs, not the exact format string

decision: Added fetch_discussions.return_value=[] to 23 mock_collector instances across test_pipeline.py and test_cli_run.py
reasoning: MagicMock.fetch_discussions() returns a MagicMock; list+MagicMock raises TypeError; all existing tests needed the stub to not break after discussions was wired into _collect_all

decision: GO — 0 Critical/High bugs; 22/22 ACs verified; 550 tests pass; coverage 96.25%; all tripwires pass
reasoning: ADR-003 null-shape order correct; ADR-002 GET regression clean; token not in body/logs/errors; inner guard re-raises AuthError/RateLimitError; R1 invariant holds; dependency audit 0 findings

decision: AC-V2-006-002 shallow TC: no exact-boundary test (days_ago==lookback_days); implementation uses < (strict), spec says 'at or after' — correct; non-blocking
reasoning: Code review confirms correct behavior; shallow TC is additive gap only, not a blocking defect

decision: ADR-003 fixture shape confirmed adequate: discussions_null=True + errors=[{type:NOT_FOUND}] matches GitHub disabled-Discussions response pattern; shape-first detection is wording-agnostic
reasoning: Active concern from _state.json resolved — no discrepancy between fixture and real GitHub response shape

decision: Integration smoke test deferred — no credentials in QA environment; accepted as [EDGE-CASE] Medium; not a GO blocker per gate rules
reasoning: All 50 mock transport tests cover every control-flow path; live smoke recommended before first production deploy
